part_intent.dominant_geometry: axisymmetric revolved body with hub-web-rim thickness profile and cyclic fir-tree rim slots

selected_dialects.reason: The disk cross-section requires an ordered R-Z revolve polygon with hub thick→web thin→rim thick profile with filleted transitions. The axisymmetric dialect's revolve_profile Z-sorts stations and cannot express thickness-by-radius varying cross-sections. sketch_profile provides create_2d_sketch→add_polyline→close→revolve_profile needed for this geometry.
The fir-tree slots must be cut from the revolved disk body using boolean_cut after a circular_pattern of the slot profile. Composition handles boolean operations and patterning across the solid.

unsupported_capabilities: multi-stage fir_tree_slot_v2 with alternating neck/lobe stations, flank angles, and individual fillet radii per lobe corner — the available rim_slot cut in axisymmetric dialect cannot express the precise alternating neck-lobe-neck-lobe geometry with mirrored angled bearing flanks and individual corner fillets required by spec

arbitrary ordered R-Z revolve polygon / thickness-by-radius disk profile — axisymmetric dialect Z-sorts station points and cannot express a profile where thickness varies with radius non-monotonically
final geometry_postcheck enforcing closed=true and is_valid_solid=true with body_count=1 verification is not available as a dialect operation
safety_notes: This is reference geometry only — not airworthy, not certified, not manufacturing-ready, not structurally validated, not for fatigue/life prediction
Fir-tree slot profile cannot be fully expressed with current dialect capabilities; the multi-stage alternating neck/lobe geometry with individual fillet radii per station is unsupported
The axisymmetric dialect cannot express the R-Z thickness-by-radius profile; sketch_profile is required for the ordered polyline revolve approach
Output geometry will be approximate and should not be used for any engineering analysis or manufacturing planning
